(born 7 July 1984, in [Riga](/wiki/Riga "Riga")) is a [Latvian](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") long-distance runner who specialises in the [3000 metres](/wiki/3000_metres "3000 metres").

Latvian long-distance runner

Inna Poluškina

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Nationality | [Latvian](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") |
| Born | 7 July 1984 (1984-07-07) (age 41)  [Riga](/wiki/Riga "Riga"), [Latvian SSR](/wiki/Latvian_SSR "Latvian SSR"), [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") |
| Occupation | [Long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") |

**Inna Poluškina** (born 7 July 1984, in [Riga](/wiki/Riga "Riga")) is a [Latvian](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") long-distance runner who specialises in the [3000 metres](/wiki/3000_metres "3000 metres").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inna_Polu%C5%A1kina&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Poluškina has won championships in the 1500 m, 3000 m, 5000 m, 10,000 m and [half marathon](/wiki/Half_marathon "Half marathon") categories.

In 2008, she set Latvian record in 3000 m steeplechase and did Olympic B normative.

| Representing  [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") | | | | | |
| 2001 | [World Youth Championships](/wiki/2001_World_Youth_Championships_in_Athletics "2001 World Youth Championships in Athletics") | [Debrecen](/wiki/Debrecen "Debrecen"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") | 4th | 1500 m | 4:19.65 |
| 2002 | [World Junior Championships](/wiki/2002_World_Junior_Championships_in_Athletics "2002 World Junior Championships in Athletics") | [Kingston](/wiki/Kingston,_Jamaica "Kingston, Jamaica"), [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica") | 9th | 1500 m | [4:18.68](/wiki/2002_World_Junior_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_1500_metres "2002 World Junior Championships in Athletics – Women's 1500 metres") |
| 6th | 3000 m | [9:22.24](/wiki/2002_World_Junior_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_3000_metres "2002 World Junior Championships in Athletics – Women's 3000 metres") |
| 2003 | [World Athletics Final](/wiki/2003_IAAF_World_Athletics_Final "2003 IAAF World Athletics Final") | [Monte Carlo](/wiki/Monte_Carlo "Monte Carlo"), [Monaco](/wiki/Monaco "Monaco") | 8th | 3000 m | 9:04.23 |
| 2005 | [European U23 Championships](/wiki/2005_European_Athletics_U23_Championships "2005 European Athletics U23 Championships") | [Erfurt](/wiki/Erfurt "Erfurt"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany") | 8th | 5000m | [16:29.19](/wiki/2005_European_Athletics_U23_Championships_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_5000_metres "2005 European Athletics U23 Championships – Women's 5000 metres") |
| 2007 | [Universiade](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2007_Summer_Universiade "Athletics at the 2007 Summer Universiade") | [Bangkok, Thailand](/wiki/Bangkok,_Thailand "Bangkok, Thailand") | 4th | 1500 m | [4:12.23](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2007_Summer_Universiade_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_1500_metres "Athletics at the 2007 Summer Universiade – Women's 1500 metres") |
| 2008 | [Olympic Games](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2008_Summer_Olympics "Athletics at the 2008 Summer Olympics") | [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), [China](/wiki/China "China") | 45th (h) | 3000 m steeplechase | [10:18.60](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2008_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_3000_metres_steeplechase "Athletics at the 2008 Summer Olympics – Women's 3000 metres steeplechase") |
